content: Pandemic preparedness encompasses the capabilities and plans needed to detect, respond to, and recover from pandemic-scale infectious disease outbreaks. The International Health Regulations (IHR 2005) is a legally binding instrument requiring 196 countries to develop core surveillance and response capacities and notify WHO of potential public health emergencies of international concern (PHEIC). Key components: early warning systems, laboratory capacity, rapid response teams, risk communication, healthcare surge capacity, and coordination mechanisms. Gaps identified during COVID-19 include: insufficient diagnostic capacity, supply chain vulnerabilities, inequitable vaccine access, fragmented global governance, and inadequate financing for preparedness.
source: WHO International Health Regulations, Global Preparedness Monitoring Board reports
